preamble: The shift between liquids [l] and [r] is extremely common and can occur in both directions. When [l] becomes [r], the process is often called rhotacism or liquid dissimilation, typically to avoid repeating identical liquid sounds in a word.

examples.note: Dissimilation: -alis becomes -aris if the stem already contains an L (e.g. solaris vs navalis).
Phonetic alternation depending on syllable position.

sources: Weiss: Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin
